Next chapter: Restructuring GitHub's bug bounty program

GitHub is making some significant changes to its bug bounty program.

Catherine Cassell

July 22, 2026
  • Share:

The security research community makes GitHub safer for everyone. That is the simple idea behind our bug bounty program.

For more than a decade, researchers from around the world have helped us find and fix vulnerabilities before they could be exploited, and we have worked hard to be a program worth their time.

What's changed and why

These changes are about reducing the noise so we can focus on the signal, and building a program that serious researchers find rewarding.

Looking ahead

We are investing in faster response times, clearer severity reasoning, and more community engagement.

We'll see you out there, and happy hacking!

Tags:

  • bug bounty
  • Security
  • security research
